sentence_reviews.text: Matrix factorization and topic models explain sparse user–item, word–document, or other observations through a smaller set of latent factors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning linear factor models; PML matrix factorization, recommender, latent-variable, and topic-model topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Latent-factor explanation of sparse matrices/documents is directly consistent with cited topics.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Matrix factorization & topic models is to connect Linear algebra, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Recommendation, ranking & personalization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning linear factor models; PML matrix factorization/recommender and topic-model/latent-variable topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Linear algebra, latent factors, and recommender use are supported by the cited topic areas, though the atlas role sentence is synthesized.
Matrix factorization is a standard recommender-system method; ranking/personalization are broader but defensible.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning linear factor models; PML matrix factorization / NMF / topic-model linear algebra context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Matrix factorization directly uses linear algebra.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning/PML factor-model, recommender, topic-model, and model-selection context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are supported; the limitation wording is generic and not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Matrix factorization & topic models rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning linear factor models; PML matrix factorization and topic-model matrix/vector representations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Matrix factorization is directly linear algebraic; topic models also use matrices/vectors, though probabilistic latent-variable structure is also central.

sentence_reviews.text: Matrix factorization & topic models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning linear factor models; PML latent-variable topic models and matrix-factorization topics.

sentence_reviews.text: Matrix factorization & topic models follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning/PML unsupervised representation/factor model topics; AIMA unsupervised learning context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Topic models and many factorization methods are unsupervised, though recommender matrix factorization can use observed preferences as supervision-like signals.

sentence_reviews.text: Matrix factorization & topic models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML probabilistic topic models / likelihood objectives and matrix-factorization objective topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Likelihood is supported for probabilistic topic models; many matrix-factorization variants optimize reconstruction/regression losses instead.

edge_reviews.notes: Matrix factorization directly requires linear algebra; topic models also use matrix/vector representations. Core is defensible for combined node.
Latent factors/topics are central; represents_with/core is supported.
Many matrix factorization/topic-model methods are unsupervised; common strength is supported.
Likelihood is central for probabilistic topic models but not all matrix factorization; relationship is partially supported, common strength is uncertain.
